title: Quercus copeyensis
intro: Quercus copeyensis, commonly known as the Copey Oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. The Copey Oak has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Quercus copeyensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the Copey Oak is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Quercus copeyensis is a species of oak tree classified within the family Fagaceae. This family includes over 600 species of trees and shrubs commonly known as beeches and oak trees. The genus Quercus, to which Quercus copeyensis belongs, is a diverse group of trees with over 600 species worldwide. Quercus copeyensis is further classified within the subgenus Lobatae, which includes white oak species found primarily in the Americas. The species Quercus copeyensis is native to Central America, specifically in the countries of Costa Rica and Panama.
uses: Quercus copeyensis, a species of oak tree native to Central America, has been found to possess several medicinal properties.
